chapter_title.ar: بَابُ حَقِّ الْمُؤْمِنِ عَلَى أَخِيهِ وَ أَدَاءِ حَقِّهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 75 – The rights of the Momin upon his brother and the fulfilling of his rights

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/45)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/45)
text: كُنْتُ عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَدَخَلَ رَجُلٌ فَسَلَّمَ فَسَأَلَهُ كَيْفَ مَنْ خَلَّفْتَ مِنْ إِخْوَانِكَ قَالَ فَأَحْسَنَ الثَّنَاءَ وَ زَكَّى وَ أَطْرَى فَقَالَ لَهُ كَيْفَ عِيَادَةُ أَغْنِيَائِهِمْ عَلَى فُقَرَائِهِمْ فَقَالَ قَلِيلَةٌ قَالَ وَ كَيْفَ مُشَاهَدَةُ أَغْنِيَائِهِمْ لِفُقَرَائِهِمْ قَالَ قَلِيلَةٌ قَالَ فَكَيْفَ صِلَةُ أَغْنِيَائِهِمْ لِفُقَرَائِهِمْ فِي ذَاتِ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَقَالَ إِنَّكَ لَتَذْكُرُ أَخْلَاقاً قَلَّ مَا هِيَ فِيمَنْ عِنْدَنَا قَالَ فَقَالَ فَكَيْفَ تَزْعُمُ هَؤُلَاءِ أَنَّهُمْ شِيعَةٌ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Usman Bin Isa, from Muhammad Bin Ajlan who said,

‘I was in the presence of Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, and a man came over. So he greeted, and he<sup>asws</sup> asked him: ‘How are the ones from your brethren whom you left behind?’ So he praised them well, and admired them. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to him: ‘How is the consolation of their rich ones upon their poor ones’. So he said, ‘Little’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And how is the observation (consideration) of their rich ones to their poor ones?’ He said, ‘Little’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So how is the helping of their rich ones to their poor ones with regards to what is in their hands?’ So he said, ‘You<sup>asws</sup> are mentioning manners. Little is what it is among the ones in our presence’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So how did you claim about them that they are Shia?’
en.sarwar: 10. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Muhammad ibn ‘Ajlan who has said the following: “Once, I was in the presence of abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) when a man came and offered the greeting of peace. The Imam asked, ‘How are your brothers (in belief) whom you have left behind?’ He praised, admired and extolled them. The Imam asked, ‘Do their wealthy ones visit their poor ones?’ He said, ‘It is very rare.’ The Imam asked, ‘Do their rich ones reach out to the poor ones?’ He said, ‘It is very rare.’ “The Imam asked, ‘Do their rich ones maintain good relations with their poor ones financially?’ He said, ‘You are speaking of the moral behavior that is practiced very rarely in our people.’ The narrator has said that the Imam then asked, ‘Why do you then think they are Shi’a (Muslims)?”’
